content: HyDE RAG’s hypothetical document generation can be used as an uncertainty probe for uncertainty-based memory selection in deep active agents: agents generate hypothetical answers to open queries to identify which existing stored memories have the highest uncertainty alignment, prioritizing only the most informative memories for RAG retrieval. This creates a self-reinforcing cycle where uncertainty drives retrieval and hypothetical generation amplifies informative uncertainty signals.
source_topics: Uncertainty-based memory sample selection for deep active agents
